text:
Just saw the film yesterday and it was pretty fantastic. Would definitely reccomend.

Even if you thought Margot Robbie was just OKAY in Suicide Squad, I guarantee you'll adore her in this film as she just let's loose with this character. The villian was really over the top, but fun and the new characters are great. The style of the film is gorgeous and really in tone with Harley Quinn and the Birds of Prey. And while there isn't a whole LOT of humor, the jokes that they do throw at you are pretty clever.

A couple downsides is that I found the female Police Officer kinda of uninteresting and there didn't feel there was a real sense of threat compared to previous DC films.

But overall, it's most definitely a great DC film. I think you'll get something nice out of it no matter what your expectations are.

text: Just watched Birds of prey and man I really wanted to like this movie a lot, and to put it simply this movie is a Let Down. I’ll say the good: the cinematography is very colorful and energetic, and the acting from Margot Robbie is spot on. Other than that this movie is a mess, it doesn’t really know what it’s even wanting to be other than saying look female empowerment, females fighting and females making the right decisions. I’m all for female empowerment if I wasn’t I wouldn’t be coming to pay for a $17 dollar movie ticket. But this movie is anti men to the point your like just why?! Black mask is a total cry baby douche and that’s everything to his character development. The story is all over the place, messy and non linear. The editing and music is kinda like what they did in suicide squad where they trick you to think your having a good time but your not. The action scenes get really tiring as well. This movie was a let down honestly and a misfire...

text:
"Do you know what a harlequin is? A Harley Quinn's role is to serve. It's nothing without a master. No one gives two shits who we are."

Fuck this okay. Harley was the Joker's Queen. "God help anyone who disrespected the queen" remember that? For those of you fucktards who don't remember allow me to jog your stupid memories.

https://youtu.be/lz2Nkal_kgc

And I know this is going to piss a lot of you off. But without joker there wouldn't be a Harley. Now don't get it twisted. I'm not trying to say that Harley is useless without the Joker that's not what I'm trying to say it all so step away from the comment section and hear me out. There is some truth to that. Why don't you all educate yourselves. Click on this link, scroll down, and then click history.

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harley_Quinn

You see? No joker no Harley. I'm not trying to say that she's worthless without him. The character has come a long way sence her first appearance in batman the animated series. I'm just trying to say that joker and Harley where literally made for each other and they're better off together than they are apart. When you go into stores what do you see? You see them on t-shirts together, you see them on posters, together you see action figures of them together. Every one I've talked to so far has agreed that what made the first suicide squad so good was the fact that joker and harley didn't hate each other. And now you are telling me they are going back to the whole abusive relationship? You people bitch and complain how joker treats harley like shit. And just when things were starting to look good for the characters you went and undid it all. Instead of building the characters up you're tearing them down. And frankly I'm sick of it. I'm sick of the DC comics. Their storyline is going nowhere it's the same shit every single time. Joker and Harley fight, Harley comes close to killing, joker but never does. They fight and fight and fight and fight. Well I can tell you right fucking now it gets old really damn quick. But it doesn't have to be that way. Anyone who disagrees get the fuck out. They have proven time after time after time that joker is fixable. There is a comic book where joker goes sane. Its literally called going sane. He isn't abusive to harley in suicide squad. He actually cares and rescued her. Twice. Onece when. She jumped into the vat of chemicals and again at the end when he breaks her out of her cell. He isn't abusive to her in arkham asylum or arkham city. And In the White knight series he goes sane again. So dont you sit there and tell me that the joker is unfixable.
So many people agreed that joker and Harleys relationship was purfect in suicide squad. It was even this great scene towards the end of the movie where Harley fantasizes about a life with Joker.

https://youtu.be/vXckaOOHNl8

Joker Goes sane, you even see him without the makeup, her and him are married and have two kids, sound familiar? I'm of course talking about the Dee Dee twins from Batman Beyond. Are we all just going to forget about that?  Are we all just going to sweep that under the rug? That's what pisses me off about this? You are just going to tear that all down. "Joker lurned to have emotion through Harley" Mark Hamill said that. He's Luke Skywalker damn it. Don't fuck with him. And he's right. Harley was there to keep the Joker in check. His partner in crime. And you destroyed that. I'll be seeing you on Rotten Tomatoes you morons. You can all kiss my ass in hell. This is why dc sucks. This is why I'm a marvel fan. Because marval is so much better than you retarded fucks. They could kill Iron Man off a thousand times and it still wouldn't be as bad as this shit. I don't know about you but I'm not going to see the new Birds of Prey movie fuck that shit you won't see a penny of my money

text:
I find it pathetic that if a white person is cast in a role of a character of a difference ethnic background, ie: Scarlett Johannson - Ghost in a Shell, then everybody goes apeshit over it calling it "white washing" but if the reverse happens then everyone is ok with it.  WHAT THE FUCK?????  It just shows you who the REAL racists are!!!!

Nick Fury turned black for Sam Jackson.  Originally a white man that fought in World War 2.
Johnny Storm turned black for Michael B. Jordan.  Originally a white man and true brother to Sue Storm.
Deadshot turned black for Will Smith.  Originally a white man.
MJ (from Spiderman) is turned into a mexican girl with rat's nest hair for Zendaya.  Originally a white redhead with the looks of a super model.

These characters change race for the movies and nobody bats a god damn eye!!!!!  Now Black Canary: a tall, WHITE, BLONDE woman is replaced with a short BLACK woman with lightened hair and everyone is ok with this?  It's obvious which direction society is going.  Diversity is just a term used for hunting down the white race for elimination.

text: Super excited for Birds of Prey!!!! I absolutely love Margot Robbie as Harley Quinn. And finally, the emancipation of Harley is here! JokerXHarley is quite possibly the most toxic romance I have ever seen in a story and I am so excited and happy that Harley is breaking away from her abuser. I also really like how Harley has replaced Barbara Gordon in this movie. I have never liked Barbara Gordon and I like her even less ever since we got the New 52 and Rebirth. The only concern I have is how they're going to portray Cassandra Cain. She's my favorite Batgirl and while I am happy they have cast an actress who kind of looks like her, (she's not exactly how I pictured Cass, but I can let it go since they're at least trying to be accurate to how the character looks in the comics, especially if she's a good actress), I fear that they won't portray Cass's character correctly seeing as she is quite possibly the biggest badass on the planet who is a nearly unbeatable fighter, reads body langauge and couldn't speak until she was in her late teens due to a psychic's intervention, and was trained to be an assassin from the time she was born due to being the daughter of David Cain and Lady Shiva. I'm so happy they are doing Harley the justice she deserves, I just hope they don't do my girl Cass dirty.

text:
christ. where to start on this mess:

1) birds of prey featuring harley quinn? but shes the biggest star and role in the movie. how is she only an "and," unless theres some purposeful irony here im missing.
2) i dont even recall harley quinn being on a birds of prey team in the last 20+ years of comic books. thanks wb/dc for following your tradition of barely trying to stay somewhat faithful to your own source material when it comes to making live action movies in a solid movie universe.
3) as a person who have been reading comic books for 20+ years, i had to look at movie posters to know who everyone else is supposed to be playing. no way in 1000 years would i have known that was black canary, renee montoya, and black mask just by looking at this trailer.
4) let me guess: a not even well done version of their final outfits will only be shown at the end, right?

5) seriously, what was wrong with just doing gotham city sirens and harley quinn instead? that at least would significantly make more sense than this.

text: Margot Robbie has absolutely nailed Harley Quinn, this just proves it. I just wonder about the other characters. If the young Asian woman is Cassandra Cain, she's been trained from birth to be the ultimate assassin by a professional killer so capable that he trained Batman. The Huntress is about a lot more than crossbows, she has more pain and loss driving her than anyone but Batman. The Black Canary is a Metahuman who can bring down a building with her Canary Cry. Renee Montoya is a cop on the GCPD before she Resigns after her Partner is killed by a dirty cop and she comes with seconds of killing the killer herself when she can't get justice-but she can't go through with it. Then, of course, there's Batman himself... But, is this even set in Gotham? I look forwards to seeing what they do with Harley in this, she looks perfect, so fingers crossed they won't white out the others.

text:
While I am not too crazy about movies where the lead role is played by a women. I have to say I am looking forward to this one. Probably more so then other super hero movies. Their was something about her and her role in Suicide Squad that was just perfect. Maybe its because I'm a guy and she was pretty sexy in it. Maybe it how unique and twisted her character was. But she was perfect for the roll. I'm not a comic book guy, so I couldn't tell you much of anything aside from what I see in the movies or from what some of my friends tell me, or news snippets and such. But I do know she did this roll perfect which left allot of us crazing for more. 
I am really hoping she makes the Joker look like a joke in this new movie. And while like many others, I did not feel the Joker in Suicide Squad was done right at all, I still appreciate it on some level. I haven't seen the new Joker movie yet, but I have been enjoying the different Jokers along the way. However I do which they could stick with one version, one movie, and just continue a story, not redue it after each movie or two.  
Either way, I am looking forward to seeing this. IT looks very promising. And I hope they didn't change her character too much as it was perfect in suicide squad.

text:
*sigh* I'm still underwhelmed and not impressed. I like the original Harley Quinn in Batman the animated series, she was intelligent, but quirky and suffered schizophrenia and Stockholm syndrome. Only turned to crime and violence to impress and get validation from the Joker.

Now in live-action we get a cookie-cutter "crazy bitch bad girl" that looks almost nothing like the original character and more like a
superficial try-hard pop music singer/imitation of Gwen Stafani *acting* "crazy" and "edgy".
I'm not impressed at all by this portrayal and the movie doesn't look all that great so far. I was vastly more impressed with Wonder Woman and Shazam!
I would *not* pay $ to see this. If/whenever it's online, I'll watch it, if I'm really bored.

text:
That Haley Quinn film will try to ride the coattails of The Joker, will have some sort of weird “damaged girls are deep” message while not being clever enough to say anything actually thought provoking or handle that kind of material of an abused females in a smart or clever way. Which has been and is still being done in very good films you’ve probably never heard of. Some of those films came out this year under the radar which I personally loved. No, it’s gonna get backlash because it’s a comic book film, which is equivalent to a AAA video game, that makes Warner Bros the big bucks, which means it matters to the mainstream because, well that’s the world we live in now. It won’t be the worst thing, just mediocre but it’s underperformance will be emphasized that much more for no real reason other than it couldn’t have possibly looked appealing to the target audience.

Money = good. Just look at those Transformers movies. Masterpieces.

Eventually when it underperforms they will blame the audience , writing stuff like “Hollywood Still Has a Sexist Bias “Males Audiences Didn’t See Harley Quinn Movie, and That’s Okay. It Wasn’t Meant for Them” type articles. They will blame “the toxic fandom” for not embracing a damaged female protagonists. It’s not because it could have been executed better, it’s not because it’s a jumbled mess of a screenplay. It’s not because it had pacing issues. No it’s the fans. It’s the audience. 

It’s not on them to make compelling films anymore. It’s you’re fault for not finding what they made interesting or worth $12. 

“You guys liked The Joker movie and not this? Wow.” Make up different variations of that quote and you got the conversation of the Harley Quinn movie. Rinse repeat 👎

text: As much as I adore Robbie in her role as Harley I just don’t think this movie will live up to the new reputation DC has made for themselves with the new Joker movie. The trailer says it all, I mean I’m not saying the movie will be bad, I’m just saying it’s following the same tone of so many other movies that have failed. I’d love to see her in a more, less mainstream and compelling version of Harley like we saw with Joker, Margot has her to a T but I just feel as though suicide squad and this movie might not do anything more for the DCU than make a few bucks. No where near on the level as Joker, it’s just a shame to let the reputation go down again and let such good acting go into such a mediocre movie.

text: DC movies don’t really feel like comic book movies anymore, at least, not in the way Marvel movies feel like comic book movies. I don’t think this is a bad thing mind you. Actually I really find it refreshing. Aquaman felt like Lord of the Rings or Star Wars, Shazam! felt like a fun 80’s or 90’s family movie or maybe like a Disney movie (albeit with some mature moments), and Birds of Prey... well I don’t know what to compare it to, but my point is that it doesn’t feel like or look like a typical comic book movie. Really happy with this new direction DC seems to be going.

text:
This sounded fun, but it looks even worse than Suicide Squad. Clearly not one person involved has any familiarity with the source material, let alone the passion to make sure the film does it justice. 

Also, I cannot understand why so many people love Margot Robbie's Harley Quinn. She's not as true to the source material as I would like, and the only thing more fake and cringy than her acting is her abysmal attempt to do Harley's iconic voice. It feels like she's so focused on trying to force the voice, slowly spitting the words out and over-anunciating, in a very strange tone that sounds nothing like Harley's iconic high-pitched voice, that the voice and the acting comes across as forced, wooden, methodical, and tired. Harley should have much more light, energy, carelessness, and speed to her, and you need to see that crazy look in her eyes. Harley is almost childlike; she is very raw, emotional, and authentic. She is far from reality. Harley Quinn doesn't ACT crazy, she IS crazy. This version just feels like an awful impression rolled in Hot Topic, but everyone loves it because she's hot and popular.

Oh my god, a feminist Harley Quinn? Harley Quinn was notoriously WEAK. Part of the tragedy of her character was that she couldn't grasp that she was in love with a man who was using her. She could be strong sometimes, but at the expense of deliberately throwing away her status as Joker's girlfriend. You had to INTRODUCE the weak Harley so that it mattered that she became strong. This movie looks... colourful, but I don't see any actual plot.

text: After seeing joker and watching this trailer all I can think is this movie is going to have the completely opposite effect of what its intention is.  Everytime Harley mentions leaving the joker all people are going to think is that's too bad because being associated with him is the coolest thing about you. Plus Margot Robbie erased everything about her portrayal of the character people gravitated to in suicide squad to appeal to the massive  imaginary group of people a small group of easily offended dorks on Twitter come off as. Todd Phillips and Joaquin pheonix really have their finger on the pulse of how people feel/what's going on in the world. This shit is like the total opposite of majority of the sentiment people are feeling in this country/worldwide (whether you lean right or left) and comes off more like they(the producers/filmmakers)really believe the world is what media spin artists and identity politicians keep trying to tell everyone it is. It's like everything that's made you roll your eyes over the last two to three years crammed into a piece of shit vanity project masquerading as a comic book movie. Luckily we have patty Jenkins and gal gadot coming with Wonder Woman 2 so there's still a strong chance for a strong female led property after this product of everything wrong with 2019.

text:
So, just to list things:
-Cassandra Cain is not silent
- *The* Cassandra Cain hides behind Harley Quinn for protection
- She seems incapable of fighting
- Harley basically forces Cassie to kill
- Apparently, fucking Black Mask is a threat to Cassandra Cain, a girl that, normally, would break every bone in his body within seconds and probably would be able to dismantle his entire criminal business in less than a day if she *really* tried.


FUCK THIS. How can this movie claim to be about emancipation, yet make what, in my opinion, is the most badass female character in the entire of DC comic history, hell maybe even comic history in general, that is known for 2 traits specifically, being silent and the best fighter in the DC universe, into a fucking non-silent scared child incapable of defending herself?
How can anyone be so stupid?

text: It’s ironical. Everybody obsessed with emancipation, you see ocean’s eleven made only with women characters, same thing for ghostbusters ... now you see a character like Harley Quinn that someone could argue if does or not deserve (or rather, be able) to keep up the plot just by herself in a screenplay. But people don’t give the character not even a chance to try. You don’t see a movie like this with a sole protagonist, women have always a huge group of co-stars (clearly other women) to take the stage with. Is it the lack of trust from the producers? The lack of inventive from the screen-writers? Or what?

text:
Holy shit, they really fucked over the other female characters to try making Harley standout/look good.

Each one of them has a really cool or unique costume in the comics. 
Huntress and Montoya (the question) have one of the most unique costumes
 in DC with a great colour palette in contrast to Harley. Here they all 
have dark boring/drab costumes, Montoya has the most stand out costume 
but standing next to Harley the stark comparison drowns out almost all 
uniqueness.

And what the hell did they do to poor Cassandra? She was a badass 
warrior and one of the most beloved batgirls. Here it looks like they 
made her into the submissive Asian female stereo type to be saved to 
make Harley look good. 

Not to mention that while Harley looks like she came from a superhero 
movie, Cassandra looks like she is on her way to shoot the remake of the
 goonies.

And I don't want to hear any bullshit that this is supposed to be set in
 a more "grounded" universe considering this is a sequel to a movie 
where a Mexican fire God, an assassin with a bright red and white 
costume, a mentally unstable girl, a crocodile man who loves watching 
BET in the sewers and an Australian hooligan using boomerangs stopped an
 Egyptian goddess, her zombie horde and her cgi brother from ending the 
world by swinging her hips.

text:
They focused a bit too much in Harley, they should give a some scenes to the characters as well, I barely made them out. 
Also, i was hoping to see the Gotham City Sirens, with Catwoman, Poison Ivy and Harley Quinn. I don't recall Harley being a Birds of Prey member in the comics, but i dont really mind. 
Some characters I made out are.
-Black Canary
I don't really liked her desigh though, i would prefer her to be a little more blonde. I hope they will be focused in her martial arts since Black Canary is one of the best martial artist in DC universe.
-Huntress
She has her crossbow, and i hope to see her, in her purple costume.
- The police girl?
I think i saw her somewhere, but i am still not sure that's her.
-Cassandra Cain?
I think she is the young Asian girl?OMG!!! I would be so happy if they really put Cass in the movie!! She really deserves some spotlight!! And i hope they treat her character well, AND make as badass as she deserves, since she is the 2nd or 3rd most skilled martial artist, even Batman can't beat her.

text:
(Helen Maxwell and Clocktower fan)


Looks more like Gotham Sirens movie. Oh well, I knew they would not use the perfect line up of Birds of Prey characters.
Black Canary is here, so are Huntress and Renee Montoya/The Question.
But, we could also have Lady Blackhawk Zinda Blake. Barabara Gordon could be introduced, so if she wasn't Batgirl yet, then have another reason for her to be there.
Hawk and Dove maybe.
Poison Ivy, let's start up her and Harley working together.
Savant, and his assistant Creote because they had a good dynamic with the rest of the Birds.

And, introduce Lady Shiva and have the Birds having to watch out for her and Black Mask.
I know Catwoman Selina Kyle can't be in it because they are using her in the Batman movies.


Birds of Prey comics during early 2000s kept my interest and had some good storylines, funny and strange ones too.  
I'm just not invested in this film.

text:
IMO I dont like this version of Harley she feels too "edgy"/hottopic/egirl
(I personally miss the BTAS annoying yet lovable ditzy ver)


I don't really like seeing Harley's character without the joker, not saying you can't make a movie where shes "moved on" but the crux of her character was that she was bad because she wanted to please "Mr. J" she wasn't a criminal beforehand, HE manipulated her into being one, and with him gone I always thought the narrative of Harley trying to adjust back into society / going straight I always found more fun / interesting /  gave more depth/sympathy to her character rather than Harley just running around doing more crimes by herself for.........money?......fun..?? idk


(also in BTAS ver. When Batman confronted Harley about the Joker and why she even likes him, she was in complete denial saying that him hurting people were just "pranks" and "jokes" and she would only try to hurt Batman because he was a threat to taking her away from her "Mr. J")

text: A little disappointed with this trailer ngl, it seems almost entirely like a Harley Quinn movie, I knew she'd be the focus and everything but not to this extent. And tbh I'm not really feeling the soundtrack, I preferred the style of the teaser with the quick cuts and EDM/Synth-pop soundtrack, the whole classical music thing seems a bit tacky like they're trying to recreate the feel of the Joker trailers. Doesn't suit Harley at all, at least not this Harley. This is supposedly the fun and bubbly Harley not the tragic mentally damaged one. And I'm not too sure why they've changed her accent to the 90's version either, seems unnecessary, and Robbie can't even pull it off. That said I'm still keeping an open mind, I didn't like the first trailers for Wonder Woman and Shazam either, and I ended up loving those movies, so we'll see.

text:
Nothing is more pathetic than people who want to ruin a character because they can't stand reality. Harley Quinn is about the realities of abuse, this idea that every female character has to stand up and kick ass is the exact same level of pathetic as thinking they should all be damsels in distress, because that's what you are making them by implying all female characters need to be saved.
It's limits female characters, it prevents them from being complete and complex characters and turns them into a pathetic fantasy escape for people who can't handle the fact that women are just people, with the same flaws and unhappy endings. They're still defined by men, their entire movies, their entire existence, is defined by men, and they are laughing stock of every man and woman who doesn't give a fuck about what the other sex wants, rebelling against something is as pathetic as pandering to it, both make you secondary to people who couldn't give a fuck about you. 
They're doing their own thing, barely aware of your existence, and you are still letting them define who you are.

This is why people HATE "strong female characters", because they're never strong, they're always examples of pathetic weakness, and an insecure pathological need to paint themselves and all other women as victims. Strong women don't do that shit, strong women just are, they are themselves, without insulting, or copying, or fighting, or rebelling, against men.

text: I honestly think that it's going to be at the very least a decent movie. Trailer's way too similar to Suicide Squad, I'll give you that. It's probably why most of haters are hating it, that and the focus on Harley Quinn. It's different than Suicide Squad's trailers in a few ways that make me more confident about the movie: we know the villain, the story is clearer, we know the main characters because there are less characters, and that trailer show a bunch of action sequences that honestly look cool. Don't think that BoP's going to be a huge hit due to Suicide Squad's huge fall but it can still a little satisfaction

text:
Margot Robbie is perfect for the role of Harley Quinn, and I love that the character is finally being adapted in movies, but I feel like Robbie is wasted on this version of the character. Unless they do something surprising. I feel like the character is overexaggerated, and even though Robbie is doing great with her performance, I think Harley Quinn is a much more interesting, nuanced character than she has been portrayed in Suicide Squad and in this film.

Also, if Poison Ivy appears in this film, I'll be very excited.
Also also, I'm very glad that this trailer didn't start with a 2-second, "This is what trailer you're about to watch" thing.

text:
I'm not sure how to feel about this movie... on the one hand I love Harley Quinn, I thought Margot did a great job bringing her to life in Suicide Squad.  

I also loved Harley in Batman TAS, and I don't have much exposure to her outside of those 2 things, but I am aware of her goings on via fan groups and nerd discussions, so I know about her on and off relationship with Ivy, and I know about the popularity of the Harley  comics which put a big spotlight focus on Harley and her exploits(naturally)...

With all that said, I feel like, with my light knowledge of the Harley comics, this movie is seemingly authentic to the art style of the comics, with all the crazy pastel colors and Harley's "new look", and all that, but maybe for the same reason I've never cared to delve into the Harley comics, this movie just doesn't seem to pull me in at all... which confuses me because I like the character, and I love Ewan Mcgregor as an actor...

Maybe it's the fact that I've been conditioned to take movies with too much emphasis on "girl power", "diversity" or other SJW nonsense that's kinda keeping my interest at bay... I mean I'm totally gonna watch this, I give every movie a fair shot regardless of how uninterested I am in it.  So i'll keep my judgement of it until after I've gotten a chance to watch it, but so far I have to say, I'm very underwhelmed... I mean as I said, I'm not the BIGGEST fan of Harley, but I do enjoy the character and Margot's portrayal of her, but this movie just seems "Meh..." to me in the same way that the show Gotham always seemed "Meh..." to me...

I hope it's great, but so far, there's nothing that's got me excited in the slightest... I'm far more excited for Joker... like BY A LOT.

text:
No Oracle...

Looks like there's no Huntress...

I don't think Harley Quinn was ever part of the Birds of Prey... Gotham City Sirens, sure, but that requires her being with Catwoman and Poison Ivy, which I think the tone of this movie would have fit better...

Whole lot of focus on women beating up men, even though men are the predominant demographic of superhero movies from DC and general audience men would likely get turned off by the aggression against men displayed in this trailer...

Rosie Perez is in it, and no one knows why...

Ewan MacGregor is Black Mask, but isn't wearing his damn mask that gave him the name...

A whole bunch of randos among Harley's "crew" and in the movie's cast itself...

The dialogue feels contrived...

The cinematography and visual style is really reminding me of Suicide Squad...

The Birds of Prey were a vigilante group/splinter cell that worked with Batman to combat crime, and that looks like it's nowhere in this film...

...it just doesn't look good.  :\

Watch this become 2020's Ghostbusters (2016) and fail.

text:
There are 2 enormous issues I have with this movie:
- Harley Quinn is the main character.
- Barbara Gordon is nowhere to be found.
Why not save this Harley story for Gotham City Sirens? I’m dying to see her & Ivy in a movie.
Why drag the Birds of Prey into this? I like the Birds of Prey. But without Barbara, whether as Batgirl or Oracle, the Birds of Prey can’t be complete.
As a big Barbara Gordon fan, I am disappointed by her absence here.

I am excited to see Huntress, though.

text: This... doesn't really seem like a Birds of Prey film. It seems like it's just Harley Quinn with a "sometimesy" Brooklyn accent which comes and goes throughout the trailer. I REALLY, *REALLY* want to root for DC films but it seems like the studio goes through comments and reviews and picks ONE THING from those reviews... then they put ALL their energy and focus on whatever that one, single thing is. For instance, people really liked the Wonder Woman film, so the studio pumps the Justice League film FULL of Wonder Woman scenes, to the point of actually neglecting other characters. And although audiences weren't crazy about the first Suicide Squad film, they really liked Harley Quinn, so what does the studio do?? They make the next film ALL ABOUT. So far I've seen very few images of the other characters which makes me worried that this film will be an overdose of Harley...which isn't necessarily a BAD thing, but based on the way things have been handled so far, it makes me uneasy.

text:
I still stand by my opinion that Margot Robbie has done a brilliant job bringing Harley to life. Yes there are things that are off, but overall she’s done well to capture Harley’s erratic, manic behavior. I really hope this movie goes into more about how toxic her relationship with Joker is. 

Because any fan of the original series will know even Harley acknowledges that she shouldn’t be in love with Joker. But she is. I love that line in Injustice where she’s like “You know how someone is terrible for you but you just can’t imagine life without them.” nothing has summed up her relationship with Joker better than that line.  She knows Joker is a horrible person but she still loves him. 

Suicide Squad made a mockery of years of comics and the original series, hopefully this movie sets the record straight.

text:
:16 secs in and i'm already pissed.... not even close to the definition or ROLE  of a Harlequin!
har·le·quin - a small duck of fast-flowing streams around the Arctic and North Pacific,
Jokes aside its more known as a mute character in traditional pantomime, typically masked and dressed in a diamond-patterned costume.
Based upon the Italian Commedia dell'arte (Which i have read!)
His role is that of a light-hearted, nimble, and astute servant (true needs a master), often acting to THWART the plans of his master, and pursuing his OWN love interest, Columbina, with wit and resourcefulness, often competing with the sterner and melancholic Pierrot. He later develops into a prototype of the romantic HERO. Harlequin inherits his physical agility and his trickster qualities, as well as his name, from a mischievous "devil" character in medieval passion plays. (Yes from wik, its the best quickest deff) 
Yes I understand this is DC's version. But don't make up your own fucking definition and role of a word that already exists! 
But on another note, this... does not look good. And i want it to. I like all the actors in this. But damn it looks.. kinda boring and silly. Gonna be all plot and forced comedy. Just show me females being bad asses, how is that so hard?? 
Okay rant over. I know people will argue, but hey thats what youtube comments are for :)

If anyone hears me and opens the door, I will come in." These words illustrates the kindness of Jesus Christ. He gently knocks on the door of our souls with His offer of peace. Jesus stands and patiently waits for us to respond.                                               

 He does not open the door Himself and force His way into our lives. He does not impose His will on ours.  Instead, He offers to all people the gift of salvation and light to guide us. To anyone who opens the door, He promises to enter. There are no other requirements or prerequisites. If you hear the voice of Jesus and His gentle knock on the door of your soul, be encouraged that He patiently waits for you and will enter if you welcome Him in.                                            

Dear one, acknowledge that you are a sinner in need of our Savior. Confess with your mouth and believe in your heart that Jesus is Lord. Welcome Abba YHWH (Father God) through Yeshua (Jesus the Christ) into your life. Receive grace by believing in Jesus Christ and what He did on the cross, His death, burial and resurrection. You will then be justified in salvation. You will be blessed abundantly with love. Jesus loves you so much, His love for you can't be adequately described in mere words. He knew you and have loved you BEFORE the creation and foundation of this earth.                  

Please be of good cheer in praying the following prayer with me.... Dear Lord Jesus, thank You for the gift of salvation and Your promise to enter when we open the door. Please help me to respond to this gift and open the door for You today. I honor You, I worship You, I praise You, and I love You. This I pray in Your Mighty Mighty name, Amen!                        May God bless you in the Glorious name of Jesus Christ our Lord and our Savior.❤️❤️❤️

text: Yeah, not sure how I feel about this. She was never part of that team, and it looks like too many actresses just to thrown in there for the hell of it. Plus, this is a terrible trailer since the narrator and focus is just on her. Didn't they learn from the mistakes of Fox and those X-Men films, the original trilogy. They too called the films the X-Men though 90% of their stories revolved exclusively around the romance between Wolverine and Jean Grey. I really really hope the film isn't centered just around Harley. If they have a good script and it indeed centers around her, please rename the film. If the trailer focuses on her but all the actresses have as much or just a little less than Harley with screen time then keep the name but give us a more accurate trailer please. This may be a cash grab film because of her popularity and if it is, I'm staying at home and telling my friends and family to do the same.

I don't really know why they called it Birds of Prey either, it seems Harley Quinn is far and away the main focus here.  I think it would've been better marketing to call it "Harley Quinn and the Birds of Prey" or something like that.  Anyway, seems like wait-and-see kind of movie.  Maybe it will be a unique, interesting action adventure, or maybe it will be another butchered would-be Guardians of the Galaxy like Suicide Squad was.

The blue sky could collapse on us, 
And the ground could crumble, 
Little matters to me if you love me, 
I don't care... 
We will have, for us, eternity,
In the blue of all the immensity,
In the sky, no more problems, 
My love, do you believe we love each other.

It really is a beautifully song.

text: So, the stylistic choices are great but that's the only postive of the movie for me. I care about sensible characterization and being respectful to the spirit of the comics, and so far the movie looks suspect on that front. Cassandra Cain is one of my favourite DC characters, so seeing her talk and accidentally kill people for fun really pisses me off, considering the history of the character. text:
Despite the endless amount of "shoulda been called Harley Quinn: The Movie" comments that will be made until the film's release, I absolutely love how they're not afraid to have fun with 80 years of comics in order to make a movie that feels fresh in this oversaturated genre. You know how many interpretations there are and how much those characters changed throughout their history? No reason to give us the same old shit over and over again. This looks far more appealing to me than some more cookie cutter family friendly content that we've seen a million times before. 


(also Harley Quinn is one of the most marketable DC characters, no shit they'll use her to sell the movie. If it does well and people love these characters, it opens the door for more movies about lesser known characters and that is a great thing)

text:
“The Joker and I broke up”
Why? I write a poem about it:
Roses are red
Violets are blue
The tattoos on his forehead
Were god awful no more jokes.

I really hope this movie is good. It looks nice but this whole universe is a disaster. Ben Affleck, Cavill, Will Smith, Leto, and Ray Fisher tapped out leaving us with Edward Cullen and Heimdall (Not bashing u Idris Elba). 

And what films have they left us? One solid movie. Shazam was great. Aquaman and Wonder Woman were pretty mediocre. I hated the effects along with Mera and Ares being lame characters. You have crumby acting and a really lazy plot. Minus Shazam, the best of these movies are at Marvels worst. And that’s sad. 

If this movie is okay, I’ll live with it. Will I see it? Sure. I don’t want movies to be bad. I just don’t like it when they’re trying to copy Marvel and not putting effort into it

text: I just can't be arsed with the DCEU anymore since watching Justice League, it just feels like it's desperately clinging to life at this point even if the more recent films have been better received. They're just throwing darts at the board now to see what sticks, there's no clear direction it seems to be going in. This is one reason why I'm looking forward to Joker this week as it has no ties to the DCEU whatsoever. Honestly the only thing in this film I'm even slightly interested in seeing is Black Canary but only if she's done properly, canary cry, batman level martial arts skills and all that jazz. The shot at 0:26 looks promising but could easily just be a visual nod to her powers in the comics while they're completely left out of the movie.  Fingers crossed I'm wrong.

Plus it would follow on nicely from their twisted love hate relationship in Suicide Squad and keep us guessing (AGAIN) as to where the Joker is throughout the entire film.
